files.title: Architecture
files.content:
# Architecture

> This is a **sample** docs bundle shipped with the dashboard. Run `build-graph.mjs` against a project to generate the real `docs-bundle.json` from its `docs/unwind/` markdown.

Unwind reverse-engineers a codebase so an AI agent can rebuild it in a different stack, preserving API contracts, business logic, and the data model.

Every documented item is tagged for rebuild prioritization:

- `[MUST]` — contracts and behavior the rebuild must preserve exactly.
- `[SHOULD]` — important but with implementation latitude.
- `[DON'T]` — incidental detail not worth carrying over.

## Layers

| Layer | What it covers |
| --- | --- |
| database | Tables, columns, relations |
| api | Endpoints and contracts |
| frontend | Pages, components, state |

files.title: Database Layer
files.content:
# Database Layer

### users [MUST] <!-- id: table:src/db/schema.ts:users -->

Core identity table. Email is unique; `id` is the foreign-key target for most relations.

### sessions [SHOULD] <!-- id: table:src/db/schema.ts:sessions -->

Server-side session store. The exact column layout can change as long as lookups stay by token.
